topic: Sweep AI Review 2026: GitHub Issue to PR Automation

key_points: Comprehensive 2026 review covering pricing, features, and use cases
Positions Sweep as a GitHub-native AI developer that converts issues into PRs
Highlights SOC 2 and ISO/IEC 27001 certifications for enterprise use
Notes limitation: GitHub-centric, less suited for GitLab/Bitbucket teams
Ranks AI dev agents including Sweep vs. Devin vs. AutoGPT vs. MetaGPT
Sweep excels for GitHub-integrated teams on routine, well-defined tasks
Devin ($500/mo) offers more autonomy but higher cost and complexity
Recommends Sweep for engineering teams wanting workflow augmentation without retooling

Sweep better for scoped, backlog-clearing tasks; Devin for complex autonomous work
Sweep zero-setup GitHub App integration is a major differentiator

OpenHands is the enterprise-grade open-source alternative to Devin

stats.fact: Sweep AI has 7,600+ GitHub stars on the sweepai/sweep repository, reflecting strong open-source adoption since its 2023 launch
Sweep AI demonstrated a 92% success rate in resolving assigned GitHub issues in controlled evaluations
Sweep AI is licensed under Apache-2.0, allowing self-hosting and code inspection — a key differentiator for security-conscious teams
CodeRabbit (primary competitor) has over 2 million repositories connected and 13 million+ PRs processed as of 2026
Sweep AI was part of Y Combinator S23 batch, validating its product-market fit early on
Devin AI (primary commercial competitor) is priced at $500/month, positioning Sweep AI free tier as highly cost-competitive for small-to-mid teams
The best AI models on SWE-Bench Pro (GPT-5, Claude Opus 4.1) resolve only ~23% of complex real-world issues, underscoring why scoped-task tools like Sweep remain relevant

angles: Sweep AI evolution: from GitHub issue-to-PR bot to full JetBrains AI coding assistant — what it means for developers in 2026
